suspects.name: Mrs. Caroline
suspects.voice: Mrs. Caroline speaks in a hushed tone, frequently mentioning her loyalty to Lady Eleanor and avoiding any discussion of the estate's financial troubles.

suspects.name: Mr. Johnson
suspects.voice: Mr. Johnson speaks in a confident, assertive manner, often interjecting with legal jargon. He avoids talking about his relationship with Lady Eleanor.

suspects.name: Mrs. Clark
suspects.deception_policy: Emphasize her care for Lady Eleanor
suspects.voice: Mrs. Clark speaks in a soft, caring tone, often talking about the importance of taking care of others but avoids discussing any medical procedures.

suspects.name: Mr. Davis
suspects.deception_policy: Claim to be an outside observer
suspects.voice: Mr. Davis speaks with a strong accent and often mentions the importance of nature, steering clear of talking about any suspicious activities.

suspects.name: Miss Green
suspects.voice: Miss Green speaks in a high-pitched voice, often using the phrase 'oh my!' She avoids any discussion of the upcoming estate sale.

locations.name: Estate Manor
locations.description: The main residence where Lady Eleanor resides.

locations.name: Library
locations.description: A room filled with books and legal documents.

locations.name: Garden
locations.description: A beautiful garden where Mr. Davis spends his time.

locations.name: Servants' Quarters
locations.description: Where the staff members of the estate live and work.

clues.description: A hidden diary revealing Lady Eleanor's true feelings about her will.
A note written in code, mentioning a secret passage to the estate manor.
Footprints leading from the garden towards the library at the time of death.
A copy of a diary, similar to the one found in the library but with additional details.
A partially used bottle of poison, near Lady Eleanor's body.

canonical_timeline.description: Miss Green was in the servants' quarters, preparing for her evening duties.
Mr. Johnson arrived at the library to study the estate's legal documents.
Mrs. Caroline, supposedly in the kitchen, was actually using a secret passage from the garden to enter the manor and commit the murder.
Mr. Davis was seen in the garden, tending to his duties but had access to secret passages.
